revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: '{{神社| 名称=若栗神社| 所在地=愛知県一宮市島村南裏山75| 位置=| 祭神=[[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]] [[応神天皇]]| 社...'
revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=若栗神社| 所在地=愛知県一宮市島村南裏山75| 位置=| 祭神=[[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]] [[応神天皇]]| 社格=式内社・旧県社| 創建=不明| }}
'''若栗神社'''（わぐりじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は県社。正式な名称は「若栗神社八幡宮」である。これは、かつて八幡宮を名乗っていたためである。しかし、若栗神社の名で呼ばれることが多い。
[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]「若栗神社」である。
== 概況 == *創建時期は[[白鳳]]年間、この地域の有力者である葉栗臣人麿が、先祖（天押帯日子命）を祀るために創建したと伝えられる。 *[[天文 (元号)|天文]]年間に仏像（[[八幡神]]）が合祀され、[[1605年]]（[[慶長]]10年）領主の兼松正吉（[[尾張藩]][[徳川義直]]家臣）が八幡宮を正式に合祀、八幡宮に改称する。 *[[1869年]]（明治2年）、元の若栗神社に改称し、郷社になるが、[[1880年]]（明治13年）に若栗神社八幡宮に改称している。 *[[1935年]]（昭和10年）、県社になる。
== 祭神 == *[[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]] :[[孝昭天皇]]の皇子。孝昭天皇はこの地に滞在したさい、この地の有力者である興津余会公の娘を妃としたという。そのとき生まれたのが天押帯日子という。 *[[応神天皇]]
== 所在地 == *愛知県一宮市島村南裏山75
== 交通機関 == *[[名鉄バス]]「若栗神社前」バス停下車、徒歩5分 **[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]][[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海道本線]][[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「川島」行き
{{DEFAULTSORT:わくりしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:一宮市]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=若栗神社| 所在地=愛知県一宮市島村南裏山75| 位置=| 祭神=[[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]] [[応神天皇]]| 社格=式内社・旧県社| 創建=不明| }}
'''若栗神社'''（わぐりじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は県社。正式な名称は「若栗神社八幡宮」である。これは、かつて八幡宮を名乗っていたためである。しかし、若栗神社の名で呼ばれることが多い。
[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]「若栗神社」である。
== 概況 == *創建時期は[[白鳳]]年間、この地域の有力者である葉栗臣人麿が、先祖（天押帯日子命）を祀るために創建したと伝えられる。 *[[天文 (元号)|天文]]年間に仏像（[[八幡神]]）が合祀され、[[1605年]]（[[慶長]]10年）領主の兼松正吉（[[尾張藩]][[徳川義直]]家臣）が八幡宮を正式に合祀、八幡宮に改称する。 *[[1869年]]（明治2年）、元の若栗神社に改称し、郷社になるが、[[1880年]]（明治13年）に若栗神社八幡宮に改称している。 *[[1935年]]（昭和10年）、県社になる。
== 祭神 == *[[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]] :[[孝昭天皇]]の皇子。孝昭天皇はこの地に滞在したさい、この地の有力者である興津余会公の娘を妃としたという。そのとき生まれたのが天押帯日子という。 *[[応神天皇]]
== 所在地 == *愛知県一宮市島村南裏山75
== 交通機関 == *[[名鉄バス]]「若栗神社前」バス停下車、徒歩すぐ **[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]][[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海道本線]][[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「川島」行き
{{DEFAULTSORT:わくりしんしや}} [[Category:愛知県の神社]] [[Category:一宮市]] {{shinto-stub}}

== 祭神 == *[[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]] :[[孝昭天皇]]の皇子。孝昭天皇はこの地に滞在したさい、この地の有力者である興津余会公の娘を妃としたという。そのとき生まれたのが天押帯日子という。 *[[応神天皇]]
== 所在地 == *愛知県一宮市島村南裏山75
== 交通機関 == *[[名鉄バス]]「若栗神社前」バス停下車、徒歩すぐ **[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]][[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海道本線]][[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「川島」行き

{{出典の明記|date=2014年10月26日 (日) 01:50 (UTC)}} {{神社| 名称=若栗神社| 画像={{画像募集中|cat=一宮市}}| 所在地=愛知県一宮市島村南裏山75| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|20|42.56|N|136|48|52.12|E|JP-23|scale:30000}}| 祭神=[[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]] [[応神天皇]]| 社格=式内社・旧県社| 創建=不明| }}
'''若栗神社'''（わぐりじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は県社。正式な名称は「若栗神社八幡宮」である。これは、かつて八幡宮を名乗っていたためである。しかし、若栗神社の名で呼ばれることが多い。
[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]「若栗神社」である。
== 概況 == *創建時期は[[白鳳]]年間、この地域の有力者である葉栗臣人麿が、先祖（天押帯日子命）を祀るために創建したと伝えられる。 *[[天文 (元号)|天文]]年間に仏像（[[八幡神]]）が合祀され、[[1605年]]（[[慶長]]10年）領主の兼松正吉（[[尾張藩]][[徳川義直]]家臣）が八幡宮を正式に合祀、八幡宮に改称する。 *[[1869年]]（明治2年）、元の若栗神社に改称し、郷社になるが、[[1880年]]（明治13年）に若栗神社八幡宮に改称している。 *[[1935年]]（昭和10年）、県社になる。
== 祭神 == *[[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]] :[[孝昭天皇]]の皇子。孝昭天皇はこの地に滞在したさい、この地の有力者である興津余会公の娘を妃としたという。そのとき生まれたのが天押帯日子という。 *[[応神天皇]]
== 交通機関 == *[[名鉄バス]]「若栗神社前」バス停下車、徒歩すぐ **[[名古屋鉄道]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]][[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海道本線]][[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「川島」行き
{{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:わくりしんしや}} [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

[[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|サムネイル|300x300ピクセル|鳥居と標柱]] [[File:若栗神社八幡宮 由緒記.jpg|thumb|由緒記|300x300ピクセル]]
'''若栗神社'''（わぐりじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は県社。正式な名称は「若栗神社八幡宮」である。これは、かつて八幡宮を名乗っていたためである。しかし、若栗神社の名で呼ばれることが多い。
[[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]「若栗神社」である。
== 概況 == 創建時期は[[白鳳]]年間、この地域の有力者である葉栗臣人麿が、先祖（天押帯日子命）を祀るために創建したと伝えられる。
[[天文 (元号)|天文]]年間に仏像（[[八幡神]]）が合祀され、[[1605年]]（[[慶長]]10年）領主の兼松正吉（[[尾張藩]][[徳川義直]]家臣）が八幡宮を正式に合祀、八幡宮に改称する。
[[1869年]]（[[明治]]2年）、元の若栗神社に改称し、郷社になるが、[[1880年]]（明治13年）に若栗神社八幡宮に改称している。
[[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）、県社になる。
== 祭神 == * [[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]] *: [[孝昭天皇]]の皇子。孝昭天皇はこの地に滞在したさい、この地の有力者である興津余会公の娘を妃としたという。そのとき生まれたのが天押帯日子という。 * [[応神天皇]]
== 祭事 == * 稚児祭（5月5日） * 例祭（10月20日）
== 境内社 == * 川島天神社 - [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]][[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]'''川嶋神社'''の論社の一つ。 * 金刀比羅社 * 平和神社
== 交通機関 == * [[名鉄バス]]「若栗神社前」バス停下車、徒歩ですぐ。 ** [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]] [[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道線 (名古屋地区)|東海道本線]] [[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「川島」行きのバスに乗車。
{{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:わくりしんしや}} [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

{{shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:わくりしんしや}} [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2014年10月26日 (日) 01:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称 = 若栗神社 | 画像 = [[ファイル:若栗神社八幡宮_拝殿.jpg|300px]]拝殿 | 所在地 = 愛知県一宮市島村南裏山75 | 緯度度 = 35| 緯度分 = 20| 緯度秒 = 42.56 | 経度度 = 136| 経度分 = 48| 経度秒 = 52.12 | 祭神 = [[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]]
[[応神天皇]] | 社格 = 式内社・旧県社 | 創建 = 不明 | 地図 = Japan Aichi }} [[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|サムネイル|300x300ピクセル|鳥居と標柱]] [[File:若栗神社八幡宮 由緒記.jpg|thumb|由緒記|300x300ピクセル]] '''若栗神社'''（わぐりじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]][[島村 (一宮市)|島村]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は県社。正式な名称は「若栗神社八幡宮」である。これは、かつて八幡宮を名乗っていたためである。しかし、若栗神社の名で呼ばれることが多い。 [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]「若栗神社」である。 == 概況 == 創建時期は[[白鳳]]年間、この地域の有力者である葉栗臣人麿が、先祖（天押帯日子命）を祀るために創建したと伝えられる。 [[天文 (元号)|天文]]年間に仏像（[[八幡神]]）が合祀され、[[1605年]]（[[慶長]]10年）領主の兼松正吉（[[尾張藩]][[徳川義直]]家臣）が八幡宮を正式に合祀、八幡宮に改称する。 [[1869年]]（[[明治]]2年）、元の若栗神社に改称し、郷社になるが、[[1880年]]（明治13年）に若栗神社八幡宮に改称している。 [[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）、県社になる。 == 祭神 == * [[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]] *: [[孝昭天皇]]の皇子。孝昭天皇はこの地に滞在したさい、この地の有力者である[[天忍男命|興津余会公]]の娘である[[世襲足媛]]を妃としたという。その後、生まれたのが天押帯日子という。 * [[応神天皇]] == 祭事 == * 稚児祭（5月5日） * 例祭（10月20日） == 境内社 == * 川島天神社 - [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]][[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]'''川嶋神社'''の論社の一つ。 * 金刀比羅社 * 平和神社 == 文化財 == ===== 一宮市指定文化財 ===== * 紙本著色兼松正吉画像 * 奉納刀3口 == 交通機関 == * [[名鉄バス]]「若栗神社前」バス停下車、徒歩ですぐ。 ** [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]] [[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道線 (名古屋地区)|東海道本線]] [[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「川島」行きのバスに乗車。 {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:わくりしんしや}} [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

{{nihongo|'''Waguri Shrine'''|若栗神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Waguri Shrine'''|若栗神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Waguri Shrine'''|若栗神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Waguri Shrine'''|若栗神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Waguri Shrine'''|若栗神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Waguri Shrine'''|若栗神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Waguri Shrine'''|若栗神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Waguri Shrine'''|若栗神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Waguri Shrine'''|若栗神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Waguri Shrine'''|若栗神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Waguri Shrine'''|若栗神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Waguri Shrine'''|若栗神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Waguri Shrine'''|若栗神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Waguri Shrine'''|若栗神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Waguri Shrine'''|若栗神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]].

revision.comment: 24 revisions imported
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2014年10月26日 (日) 01:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称 = 若栗神社 | 画像 = [[ファイル:若栗神社八幡宮_拝殿.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 | 所在地 = [[愛知県]][[一宮市]]島村南裏山75 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = [[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]]
[[応神天皇]] | 社格 = [[式内社]]・旧[[県社]] | 創建 = 不明 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=若栗神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|サムネイル|250px|鳥居と標柱]] [[File:若栗神社八幡宮 由緒記.jpg|thumb|250px|由緒記]] '''若栗神社'''（わぐりじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]][[島村 (一宮市)|島村]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は県社。正式な名称は「若栗神社八幡宮」である。これは、かつて八幡宮を名乗っていたためである。しかし、若栗神社の名で呼ばれることが多い。 [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]「若栗神社」である。 == 概況 == 創建時期は[[白鳳]]年間、この地域の有力者である葉栗臣人麿が、先祖（天押帯日子命）を祀るために創建したと伝えられる。 [[天文 (元号)|天文]]年間に仏像（[[八幡神]]）が合祀され、[[1605年]]（[[慶長]]10年）領主の兼松正吉（[[尾張藩]][[徳川義直]]家臣）が八幡宮を正式に合祀、八幡宮に改称する。 [[1869年]]（[[明治]]2年）、元の若栗神社に改称し、郷社になるが、[[1880年]]（明治13年）に若栗神社八幡宮に改称している。 [[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）、県社になる。 == 祭神 == * [[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]] *: [[孝昭天皇]]の皇子。孝昭天皇はこの地に滞在したさい、この地の有力者である[[天忍男命|興津余会公]]の娘である[[世襲足媛]]を妃としたという。その後、生まれたのが天押帯日子という。 * [[応神天皇]] == 祭事 == * 稚児祭（5月5日） * 例祭（10月20日） == 境内社 == * 川島天神社 - [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]][[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]'''川嶋神社'''の論社の一つ。 * 金刀比羅社 * 平和神社 == 文化財 == === 一宮市指定文化財 === * 紙本著色兼松正吉画像 * 奉納刀3口 == 交通機関 == * [[名鉄バス]]「若栗神社前」バス停下車、徒歩ですぐ。 ** [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]] [[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道線 (名古屋地区)|東海道本線]] [[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「川島」行きのバスに乗車。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:わくりしんしや}} [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

[[応神天皇]] | 社格 = [[式内社]]・旧[[県社]] | 創建 = 不明 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=若栗神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|サムネイル|250px|鳥居と標柱]] [[File:若栗神社八幡宮 由緒記.jpg|thumb|250px|由緒記]] '''若栗神社'''（わぐりじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]][[島村 (一宮市)|島村]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は県社。正式な名称は「若栗神社八幡宮」である。これは、かつて八幡宮を名乗っていたためである。しかし、若栗神社の名で呼ばれることが多い。 [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]「若栗神社」である。 == 概況 == 創建時期は[[白鳳]]年間、この地域の有力者である葉栗臣人麿が、先祖（天押帯日子命）を祀るために創建したと伝えられる。 [[天文 (元号)|天文]]年間に仏像（[[八幡神]]）が合祀され、[[1605年]]（[[慶長]]10年）領主の兼松正吉（[[尾張藩]][[徳川義直]]家臣）が八幡宮を正式に合祀、八幡宮に改称する。 [[1869年]]（[[明治]]2年）、元の若栗神社に改称し、郷社になるが、[[1880年]]（明治13年）に若栗神社八幡宮に改称している。 [[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）、県社になる。 == 祭神 == * [[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]] *: [[孝昭天皇]]の皇子。孝昭天皇はこの地に滞在したさい、この地の有力者である[[天忍男命|興津余会公]]の娘である[[世襲足媛]]を妃としたという。その後、生まれたのが天押帯日子という。 * [[応神天皇]] == 祭事 == * 稚児祭（5月5日） * 例祭（10月20日） == 境内社 == * 川島天神社 - [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]][[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]'''川嶋神社'''の論社の一つ。 * 金刀比羅社 * 平和神社 == 文化財 == === 一宮市指定文化財 === * 紙本著色兼松正吉画像 * 奉納刀3口 == 交通機関 == * [[名鉄バス]]「若栗神社前」バス停下車、徒歩ですぐ。 ** [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]] [[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道線 (名古屋地区)|東海道本線]] [[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「川島」行きのバスに乗車。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:わくりしんしや}} [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

[[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|若栗神社|version=107181416|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年10月26日 (日) 01:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称 = 若栗神社 | 画像 = [[ファイル:若栗神社八幡宮_拝殿.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 | 所在地 = [[愛知県]][[一宮市]]島村南裏山75 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = [[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]]
[[応神天皇]] | 社格 = [[式内社]]・旧[[県社]] | 創建 = 不明 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=若栗神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|サムネイル|250px|鳥居と標柱]] [[File:若栗神社八幡宮 由緒記.jpg|thumb|250px|由緒記]] '''若栗神社'''（わぐりじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]][[島村 (一宮市)|島村]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は県社。正式な名称は「若栗神社八幡宮」である。これは、かつて八幡宮を名乗っていたためである。しかし、若栗神社の名で呼ばれることが多い。 [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]「若栗神社」である。 == 概況 == 創建時期は[[白鳳]]年間、この地域の有力者である葉栗臣人麿が、先祖（天押帯日子命）を祀るために創建したと伝えられる。 [[天文 (元号)|天文]]年間に仏像（[[八幡神]]）が合祀され、[[1605年]]（[[慶長]]10年）領主の兼松正吉（[[尾張藩]][[徳川義直]]家臣）が八幡宮を正式に合祀、八幡宮に改称する。 [[1869年]]（[[明治]]2年）、元の若栗神社に改称し、郷社になるが、[[1880年]]（明治13年）に若栗神社八幡宮に改称している。 [[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）、県社になる。 == 祭神 == * [[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]] *: [[孝昭天皇]]の皇子。孝昭天皇はこの地に滞在したさい、この地の有力者である[[天忍男命|興津余会公]]の娘である[[世襲足媛]]を妃としたという。その後、生まれたのが天押帯日子という。 * [[応神天皇]] == 祭事 == * 稚児祭（5月5日） * 例祭（10月20日） == 境内社 == * 川島天神社 - [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]][[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]'''川嶋神社'''の論社の一つ。 * 金刀比羅社 * 平和神社 == 文化財 == === 一宮市指定文化財 === * 紙本著色兼松正吉画像 * 奉納刀3口 == 交通機関 == * [[名鉄バス]]「若栗神社前」バス停下車、徒歩ですぐ。 ** [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]] [[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道線 (名古屋地区)|東海道本線]] [[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「川島」行きのバスに乗車。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:わくりしんしや}} [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] ==End Japanese==

[[応神天皇]] | 社格 = [[式内社]]・旧[[県社]] | 創建 = 不明 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=若栗神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|サムネイル|250px|鳥居と標柱]] [[File:若栗神社八幡宮 由緒記.jpg|thumb|250px|由緒記]] '''若栗神社'''（わぐりじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]][[島村 (一宮市)|島村]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は県社。正式な名称は「若栗神社八幡宮」である。これは、かつて八幡宮を名乗っていたためである。しかし、若栗神社の名で呼ばれることが多い。 [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]「若栗神社」である。 == 概況 == 創建時期は[[白鳳]]年間、この地域の有力者である葉栗臣人麿が、先祖（天押帯日子命）を祀るために創建したと伝えられる。 [[天文 (元号)|天文]]年間に仏像（[[八幡神]]）が合祀され、[[1605年]]（[[慶長]]10年）領主の兼松正吉（[[尾張藩]][[徳川義直]]家臣）が八幡宮を正式に合祀、八幡宮に改称する。 [[1869年]]（[[明治]]2年）、元の若栗神社に改称し、郷社になるが、[[1880年]]（明治13年）に若栗神社八幡宮に改称している。 [[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）、県社になる。 == 祭神 == * [[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]] *: [[孝昭天皇]]の皇子。孝昭天皇はこの地に滞在したさい、この地の有力者である[[天忍男命|興津余会公]]の娘である[[世襲足媛]]を妃としたという。その後、生まれたのが天押帯日子という。 * [[応神天皇]] == 祭事 == * 稚児祭（5月5日） * 例祭（10月20日） == 境内社 == * 川島天神社 - [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]][[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]'''川嶋神社'''の論社の一つ。 * 金刀比羅社 * 平和神社 == 文化財 == === 一宮市指定文化財 === * 紙本著色兼松正吉画像 * 奉納刀3口 == 交通機関 == * [[名鉄バス]]「若栗神社前」バス停下車、徒歩ですぐ。 ** [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]] [[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道線 (名古屋地区)|東海道本線]] [[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「川島」行きのバスに乗車。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] ==End Japanese==

[[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[JA:若栗神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616546}} [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] {{translated page|ja|若栗神社|version=107181416|comment=Imported full ja history}}[[Category:Automerged Japanese text]] == Japanese Wikipedia content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年10月26日 (日) 01:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称 = 若栗神社 | 画像 = [[ファイル:若栗神社八幡宮_拝殿.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 | 所在地 = [[愛知県]][[一宮市]]島村南裏山75 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = [[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]]
[[応神天皇]] | 社格 = [[式内社]]・旧[[県社]] | 創建 = 不明 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=若栗神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|サムネイル|250px|鳥居と標柱]] [[File:若栗神社八幡宮 由緒記.jpg|thumb|250px|由緒記]] '''若栗神社'''（わぐりじんじゃ）は、[[愛知県]][[一宮市]][[島村 (一宮市)|島村]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は県社。正式な名称は「若栗神社八幡宮」である。これは、かつて八幡宮を名乗っていたためである。しかし、若栗神社の名で呼ばれることが多い。 [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]]の[[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]「若栗神社」である。 == 概況 == 創建時期は[[白鳳]]年間、この地域の有力者である葉栗臣人麿が、先祖（天押帯日子命）を祀るために創建したと伝えられる。 [[天文 (元号)|天文]]年間に仏像（[[八幡神]]）が合祀され、[[1605年]]（[[慶長]]10年）領主の兼松正吉（[[尾張藩]][[徳川義直]]家臣）が八幡宮を正式に合祀、八幡宮に改称する。 [[1869年]]（[[明治]]2年）、元の若栗神社に改称し、郷社になるが、[[1880年]]（明治13年）に若栗神社八幡宮に改称している。 [[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）、県社になる。 == 祭神 == * [[天足彦国押人命|天押帯日子命]] *: [[孝昭天皇]]の皇子。孝昭天皇はこの地に滞在したさい、この地の有力者である[[天忍男命|興津余会公]]の娘である[[世襲足媛]]を妃としたという。その後、生まれたのが天押帯日子という。 * [[応神天皇]] == 祭事 == * 稚児祭（5月5日） * 例祭（10月20日） == 境内社 == * 川島天神社 - [[延喜式神名帳|式内社]][[尾張国]][[葉栗郡]]'''川嶋神社'''の論社の一つ。 * 金刀比羅社 * 平和神社 == 文化財 == === 一宮市指定文化財 === * 紙本著色兼松正吉画像 * 奉納刀3口 == 交通機関 == * [[名鉄バス]]「若栗神社前」バス停下車、徒歩ですぐ。 ** [[名古屋鉄道|名鉄]][[名鉄名古屋本線|名古屋本線]]・[[名鉄尾西線|尾西線]] [[名鉄一宮駅]]、[[東海旅客鉄道|JR]][[東海道線 (名古屋地区)|東海道本線]] [[尾張一宮駅]]（[[名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル]]3番のりば）より「川島」行きのバスに乗車。 == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} ==End Japanese== [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

(Japan) (P3225) == * 1180005010190 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市島村南裏山75', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:若栗神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616546}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年10月26日 (日) 01:50 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称 = 若栗神社 | 画像 = [[ファイル:若栗神社八幡宮_拝殿.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 | 所在地 = {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}島村南裏山75 | 位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | 祭神 = {{jalink|天足彦国押人命|Q11443593|天押帯日子命}}
{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} | 社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|県社}} | 創建 = 不明 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=若栗神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|サムネイル|250px|鳥居と標柱]] [[File:若栗神社八幡宮 由緒記.jpg|thumb|250px|由緒記]] '''若栗神社'''（わぐりじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|島村 (一宮市)|Q128848556|島村}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は県社。正式な名称は「若栗神社八幡宮」である。これは、かつて八幡宮を名乗っていたためである。しかし、若栗神社の名で呼ばれることが多い。 {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|葉栗郡|Q1032507}}「若栗神社」である。 === 概況 === 創建時期は{{jalink|白鳳|Q11580612}}年間、この地域の有力者である葉栗臣人麿が、先祖（天押帯日子命）を祀るために創建したと伝えられる。 {{jalink|天文 (元号)|Q1192853|天文}}年間に仏像（{{jalink|八幡神|Q261637}}）が合祀され、{{jalink|1605年|Q6741}}（{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}10年）領主の兼松正吉（{{jalink|尾張藩|Q1062072}}{{jalink|徳川義直|Q1136186}}家臣）が八幡宮を正式に合祀、八幡宮に改称する。 {{jalink|1869年|Q7720}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}2年）、元の若栗神社に改称し、郷社になるが、{{jalink|1880年|Q7806}}（明治13年）に若栗神社八幡宮に改称している。 {{jalink|1935年|Q18658}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}10年）、県社になる。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|天足彦国押人命|Q11443593|天押帯日子命}} *: {{jalink|孝昭天皇|Q312841}}の皇子。孝昭天皇はこの地に滞在したさい、この地の有力者である{{jalink|天忍男命|Q97613650|興津余会公}}の娘である{{jalink|世襲足媛|Q10869205}}を妃としたという。その後、生まれたのが天押帯日子という。 * {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} === 祭事 === * 稚児祭（5月5日） * 例祭（10月20日） === 境内社 === * 川島天神社 - {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|葉栗郡|Q1032507}}'''川嶋神社'''の論社の一つ。 * 金刀比羅社 * 平和神社 === 文化財 === === 一宮市指定文化財 === * 紙本著色兼松正吉画像 * 奉納刀3口 === 交通機関 === * {{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「若栗神社前」バス停下車、徒歩ですぐ。 ** {{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850|名鉄}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}} {{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海旅客鉄道|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|東海道線 (名古屋地区)|Q11235139|東海道本線}} {{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「川島」行きのバスに乗車。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:わくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

{{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} | 社格 = {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|県社}} | 創建 = 不明 | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=若栗神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|サムネイル|250px|鳥居と標柱]] [[File:若栗神社八幡宮 由緒記.jpg|thumb|250px|由緒記]] '''若栗神社'''（わぐりじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|島村 (一宮市)|Q128848556|島村}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。旧{{jalink|社格|Q10444029}}は県社。正式な名称は「若栗神社八幡宮」である。これは、かつて八幡宮を名乗っていたためである。しかし、若栗神社の名で呼ばれることが多い。 {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}の{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|葉栗郡|Q1032507}}「若栗神社」である。 === 概況 === 創建時期は{{jalink|白鳳|Q11580612}}年間、この地域の有力者である葉栗臣人麿が、先祖（天押帯日子命）を祀るために創建したと伝えられる。 {{jalink|天文 (元号)|Q1192853|天文}}年間に仏像（{{jalink|八幡神|Q261637}}）が合祀され、{{jalink|1605年|Q6741}}（{{jalink|慶長|Q1328185}}10年）領主の兼松正吉（{{jalink|尾張藩|Q1062072}}{{jalink|徳川義直|Q1136186}}家臣）が八幡宮を正式に合祀、八幡宮に改称する。 {{jalink|1869年|Q7720}}（{{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}2年）、元の若栗神社に改称し、郷社になるが、{{jalink|1880年|Q7806}}（明治13年）に若栗神社八幡宮に改称している。 {{jalink|1935年|Q18658}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}10年）、県社になる。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|天足彦国押人命|Q11443593|天押帯日子命}} *: {{jalink|孝昭天皇|Q312841}}の皇子。孝昭天皇はこの地に滞在したさい、この地の有力者である{{jalink|天忍男命|Q97613650|興津余会公}}の娘である{{jalink|世襲足媛|Q10869205}}を妃としたという。その後、生まれたのが天押帯日子という。 * {{jalink|応神天皇|Q317997}} === 祭事 === * 稚児祭（5月5日） * 例祭（10月20日） === 境内社 === * 川島天神社 - {{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932|式内社}}{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|葉栗郡|Q1032507}}'''川嶋神社'''の論社の一つ。 * 金刀比羅社 * 平和神社 === 文化財 === === 一宮市指定文化財 === * 紙本著色兼松正吉画像 * 奉納刀3口 === 交通機関 === * {{jalink|名鉄バス|Q6810397}}「若栗神社前」バス停下車、徒歩ですぐ。 ** {{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850|名鉄}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}}・{{jalink|名鉄尾西線|Q901005|尾西線}} {{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}、{{jalink|東海旅客鉄道|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|東海道線 (名古屋地区)|Q11235139|東海道本線}} {{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}（{{jalink|名鉄一宮駅バスターミナル}}3番のりば）より「川島」行きのバスに乗車。 === 脚注 === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{デフォルトソート:わくりしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Autogenerated pages with jawiki interwikis, possibly accidentally overwritten]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:need translation]]

needed|date=October 26, 2014 (Sun) 01:50 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Waguri Shrine | Image = [[File:Waguri Shrine Hachimangu Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya City|Q270810}} Shimamura Minami-Urayama 75 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = {{jalink|Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} · Former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} | Established = Unknown | Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Waguri Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|thumbnail|250px|Torii gate and marker pillar]] [[File:Waguri Shrine Hachimangu Origin Record.jpg|thumb|250px|Origin record]] '''Waguri Shrine''' (わぐりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Shimamura (Ichinomiya City)|Q128848556|Shimamura}}, {{jalink|Ichinomiya City|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was Prefectural Shrine. The official name is “Waguri Shrine Hachimangū.” This is because it once bore the name Hachimangū. However, it is more commonly called Waguri Shrine. It is the “Waguri Shrine” of {{jalink|Haguri District|Q1032507}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}} as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. === Overview === The founding date is said to be during the {{jalink|Hakuhō period|Q11580612}}, when a local powerful figure of this region, Haguri no Omi Hitomaro, established the shrine to enshrine his ancestor (Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto). During the {{jalink|Tenbun era|Q1192853}}, a Buddhist statue ({{jalink|Hachiman deity|Q261637}}) was jointly enshrined, and in {{jalink|1605|Q6741}} (Keichō 10), the domain lord Kanematsu Masakichi (a retainer of {{jalink|Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186}} of the {{jalink|Owari Domain|Q1062072}}) formally enshrined Hachimangū, and the shrine was renamed Hachimangū. In {{jalink|1869|Q7720}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed back to Waguri Shrine and became a village shrine, but in {{jalink|1880|Q7806}} (Meiji 13) it was again renamed Waguri Shrine Hachimangū. In {{jalink|1935|Q18658}} (Shōwa 10), it became a Prefectural Shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto}} *: A prince of {{jalink|Emperor Kōshō|Q312841}}. It is said that when Emperor Kōshō stayed in this area, he took as consort {{jalink|Yotsugi Tarashi-hime|Q10869205}}, the daughter of {{jalink|Ame-no-Oshin-O no Mikoto|Q97613650|Okitsu Yoae no Kimi}}, a local powerful figure. Their child was Ame-oshitara-hiko. * {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} === Festivals === * Chigo Festival (May 5) * Annual Festival (October 20) === Auxiliary shrines === * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine – One of the candidate sites (ronsha) for '''Kawashima Shrine''' of {{jalink|Haguri District|Q1032507}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}}. * Kotohira Shrine * Heiwa Shrine === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Ichinomiya City === * Colored portrait on paper of Kanematsu Masakichi * Three donated swords === Transportation === * Get off at the {{jalink|Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397}} “Waguri Shrine-mae” bus stop; the shrine is just a short walk away. ** From {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}} or {{jalink|Bisai Line|Q901005}}, or from {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} on the {{jalink|Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139}} of {{jalink|Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679}}, take a bus bound for “Kawashima” from platform 3 of the Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakurishinsha}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:need translation]]

needed|date=October 26, 2014 (Sun) 01:50 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Waguri Shrine | Image = [[File:Waguri Shrine Hachimangu Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya City|Q270810}} Shimamura Minami-Urayama 75 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = {{jalink|Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} · Former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} | Established = Unknown | Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Waguri Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|thumbnail|250px|Torii gate and marker pillar]] [[File:Waguri Shrine Hachimangu Origin Record.jpg|thumb|250px|Origin record]] '''Waguri Shrine''' (わぐりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Shimamura (Ichinomiya City)|Q128848556|Shimamura}}, {{jalink|Ichinomiya City|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was Prefectural Shrine. The official name is “Waguri Shrine Hachimangū.” This is because it once bore the name Hachimangū. However, it is more commonly called Waguri Shrine. It is the “Waguri Shrine” of {{jalink|Haguri District|Q1032507}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}} as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. === Overview === The founding date is said to be during the {{jalink|Hakuhō period|Q11580612}}, when a local powerful figure of this region, Haguri no Omi Hitomaro, established the shrine to enshrine his ancestor (Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto). During the {{jalink|Tenbun era|Q1192853}}, a Buddhist statue ({{jalink|Hachiman deity|Q261637}}) was jointly enshrined, and in {{jalink|1605|Q6741}} (Keichō 10), the domain lord Kanematsu Masakichi (a retainer of {{jalink|Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186}} of the {{jalink|Owari Domain|Q1062072}}) formally enshrined Hachimangū, and the shrine was renamed Hachimangū. In {{jalink|1869|Q7720}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed back to Waguri Shrine and became a village shrine, but in {{jalink|1880|Q7806}} (Meiji 13) it was again renamed Waguri Shrine Hachimangū. In {{jalink|1935|Q18658}} (Shōwa 10), it became a Prefectural Shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto}} *: A prince of {{jalink|Emperor Kōshō|Q312841}}. It is said that when Emperor Kōshō stayed in this area, he took as consort {{jalink|Yotsugi Tarashi-hime|Q10869205}}, the daughter of {{jalink|Ame-no-Oshin-O no Mikoto|Q97613650|Okitsu Yoae no Kimi}}, a local powerful figure. Their child was Ame-oshitara-hiko. * {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} === Festivals === * Chigo Festival (May 5) * Annual Festival (October 20) === Auxiliary shrines === * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine – One of the candidate sites (ronsha) for '''Kawashima Shrine''' of {{jalink|Haguri District|Q1032507}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}}. * Kotohira Shrine * Heiwa Shrine === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Ichinomiya City === * Colored portrait on paper of Kanematsu Masakichi * Three donated swords === Transportation === * Get off at the {{jalink|Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397}} “Waguri Shrine-mae” bus stop; the shrine is just a short walk away. ** From {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}} or {{jalink|Bisai Line|Q901005}}, or from {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} on the {{jalink|Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139}} of {{jalink|Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679}}, take a bus bound for “Kawashima” from platform 3 of the Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakurishinsha}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

needed|date=October 26, 2014 (Sun) 01:50 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Waguri Shrine | Image = [[File:Waguri Shrine Hachimangu Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya City|Q270810}} Shimamura Minami-Urayama 75 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = {{jalink|Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} | Shrine rank = {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} · Former {{jalink|Prefectural Shrine}} | Established = Unknown | Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Waguri Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|thumbnail|250px|Torii gate and marker pillar]] [[File:Waguri Shrine Hachimangu Origin Record.jpg|thumb|250px|Origin record]] '''Waguri Shrine''' (わぐりじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} located in {{jalink|Shimamura (Ichinomiya City)|Q128848556|Shimamura}}, {{jalink|Ichinomiya City|Q270810}}, {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}. Its former {{jalink|shrine rank|Q10444029}} was Prefectural Shrine. The official name is “Waguri Shrine Hachimangū.” This is because it once bore the name Hachimangū. However, it is more commonly called Waguri Shrine. It is the “Waguri Shrine” of {{jalink|Haguri District|Q1032507}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}} as a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}. === Overview === The founding date is said to be during the {{jalink|Hakuhō period|Q11580612}}, when a local powerful figure of this region, Haguri no Omi Hitomaro, established the shrine to enshrine his ancestor (Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto). During the {{jalink|Tenbun era|Q1192853}}, a Buddhist statue ({{jalink|Hachiman deity|Q261637}}) was jointly enshrined, and in {{jalink|1605|Q6741}} (Keichō 10), the domain lord Kanematsu Masakichi (a retainer of {{jalink|Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186}} of the {{jalink|Owari Domain|Q1062072}}) formally enshrined Hachimangū, and the shrine was renamed Hachimangū. In {{jalink|1869|Q7720}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed back to Waguri Shrine and became a village shrine, but in {{jalink|1880|Q7806}} (Meiji 13) it was again renamed Waguri Shrine Hachimangū. In {{jalink|1935|Q18658}} (Shōwa 10), it became a Prefectural Shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto}} *: A prince of {{jalink|Emperor Kōshō|Q312841}}. It is said that when Emperor Kōshō stayed in this area, he took as consort {{jalink|Yotsugi Tarashi-hime|Q10869205}}, the daughter of {{jalink|Ame-no-Oshin-O no Mikoto|Q97613650|Okitsu Yoae no Kimi}}, a local powerful figure. Their child was Ame-oshitara-hiko. * {{jalink|Emperor Ōjin|Q317997}} === Festivals === * Chigo Festival (May 5) * Annual Festival (October 20) === Auxiliary shrines === * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine – One of the candidate sites (ronsha) for '''Kawashima Shrine''' of {{jalink|Haguri District|Q1032507}}, {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}, listed in the {{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|Engishiki}}. * Kotohira Shrine * Heiwa Shrine === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Ichinomiya City === * Colored portrait on paper of Kanematsu Masakichi * Three donated swords === Transportation === * Get off at the {{jalink|Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397}} “Waguri Shrine-mae” bus stop; the shrine is just a short walk away. ** From {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}} on the {{jalink|Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838}} or {{jalink|Bisai Line|Q901005}}, or from {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}} on the {{jalink|Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139}} of {{jalink|Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679}}, take a bus bound for “Kawashima” from platform 3 of the Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakurishinsha}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

During the {{ill|lt=Tenbun era|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853}}, a Buddhist statue ({{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637}}) was jointly enshrined, and in {{ill|lt=1605|Q6741|WD=Q6741}} (Keichō 10), the domain lord Kanematsu Masakichi (a retainer of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186|WD=Q1136186}} of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072}}) formally enshrined Hachimangū, and the shrine was renamed Hachimangū. In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed back to Waguri Shrine and became a village shrine, but in {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}} (Meiji 13) it was again renamed Waguri Shrine Hachimangū. In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658}} (Shōwa 10), it became a Prefectural Shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|lt=Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto}} *: A prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841}}. It is said that when Emperor Kōshō stayed in this area, he took as consort {{ill|lt=Yotsugi Tarashi-hime|Q10869205|WD=Q10869205}}, the daughter of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshin-O no Mikoto|Q97613650|WD=Q97613650|lt=Okitsu Yoae no Kimi}}, a local powerful figure. Their child was Ame-oshitara-hiko. * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} === Festivals === * Chigo Festival (May 5) * Annual Festival (October 20) === Auxiliary shrines === * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine – One of the candidate sites (ronsha) for '''Kawashima Shrine''' of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}. * Kotohira Shrine * Heiwa Shrine === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Ichinomiya City === * Colored portrait on paper of Kanematsu Masakichi * Three donated swords === Transportation === * Get off at the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397}} “Waguri Shrine-mae” bus stop; the shrine is just a short walk away. ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838}} or {{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|WD=Q11235139}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679}}, take a bus bound for “Kawashima” from platform 3 of the Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakurishinsha}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658}} (Shōwa 10), it became a Prefectural Shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|lt=Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto}} *: A prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841}}. Their child was Ame-oshitara-hiko. * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} === Festivals === * Chigo Festival (May 5) * Annual Festival (October 20) === Auxiliary shrines === * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine – One of the candidate sites (ronsha) for '''Kawashima Shrine''' of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}. * Kotohira Shrine * Heiwa Shrine === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Ichinomiya City === * Colored portrait on paper of Kanematsu Masakichi * Three donated swords === Transportation === * Get off at the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397}} “Waguri Shrine-mae” bus stop; the shrine is just a short walk away. ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838}} or {{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|WD=Q11235139}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679}}, take a bus bound for “Kawashima” from platform 3 of the Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakurishinsha}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|WD=Q270810}} Shimamura Minami-Urayama 75 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|lt=Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} · Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | Established = Unknown | Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Waguri Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|thumbnail|250px|Torii gate and marker pillar]] [[File:Waguri Shrine Hachimangu Origin Record.jpg|thumb|250px|Origin record]] '''Waguri Shrine''' (わぐりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Shimamura (Ichinomiya City)|Q128848556|WD=Q128848556|lt=Shimamura}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was Prefectural Shrine. The official name is “Waguri Shrine Hachimangū.” This is because it once bore the name Hachimangū. However, it is more commonly called Waguri Shrine. It is the “Waguri Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}} as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. === Overview === The founding date is said to be during the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}}, when a local powerful figure of this region, Haguri no Omi Hitomaro, established the shrine to enshrine his ancestor (Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto). During the {{ill|lt=Tenbun era|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853}}, a Buddhist statue ({{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637}}) was jointly enshrined, and in {{ill|lt=1605|Q6741|WD=Q6741}} (Keichō 10), the domain lord Kanematsu Masakichi (a retainer of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186|WD=Q1136186}} of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072}}) formally enshrined Hachimangū, and the shrine was renamed Hachimangū. In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed back to Waguri Shrine and became a village shrine, but in {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}} (Meiji 13) it was again renamed Waguri Shrine Hachimangū. In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658}} (Shōwa 10), it became a Prefectural Shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|lt=Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto}} *: A prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841}}. It is said that when Emperor Kōshō stayed in this area, he took as consort {{ill|lt=Yotsugi Tarashi-hime|Q10869205|WD=Q10869205}}, the daughter of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshin-O no Mikoto|Q97613650|WD=Q97613650|lt=Okitsu Yoae no Kimi}}, a local powerful figure. Their child was Ame-oshitara-hiko. * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} === Festivals === * Chigo Festival (May 5) * Annual Festival (October 20) === Auxiliary shrines === * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine – One of the candidate sites (ronsha) for '''Kawashima Shrine''' of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}. * Kotohira Shrine * Heiwa Shrine === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Ichinomiya City === * Colored portrait on paper of Kanematsu Masakichi * Three donated swords === Transportation === * Get off at the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397}} “Waguri Shrine-mae” bus stop; the shrine is just a short walk away. ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838}} or {{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|WD=Q11235139}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679}}, take a bus bound for “Kawashima” from platform 3 of the Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakurishinsha}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

[[JA:若栗神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616546}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=October 26, 2014 (Sun) 01:50 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Waguri Shrine | Image = [[File:Waguri Shrine Hachimangu Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|WD=Q270810}} Shimamura Minami-Urayama 75 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|lt=Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} · Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | Established = Unknown | Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Waguri Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|thumbnail|250px|Torii gate and marker pillar]] [[File:Waguri Shrine Hachimangu Origin Record.jpg|thumb|250px|Origin record]] '''Waguri Shrine''' (わぐりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Shimamura (Ichinomiya City)|Q128848556|WD=Q128848556|lt=Shimamura}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|WD=Q270810}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029}} was Prefectural Shrine. The official name is “Waguri Shrine Hachimangū.” This is because it once bore the name Hachimangū. However, it is more commonly called Waguri Shrine. It is the “Waguri Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}} as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}. === Overview === The founding date is said to be during the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612}}, when a local powerful figure of this region, Haguri no Omi Hitomaro, established the shrine to enshrine his ancestor (Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto). During the {{ill|lt=Tenbun era|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853}}, a Buddhist statue ({{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637}}) was jointly enshrined, and in {{ill|lt=1605|Q6741|WD=Q6741}} (Keichō 10), the domain lord Kanematsu Masakichi (a retainer of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186|WD=Q1136186}} of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072}}) formally enshrined Hachimangū, and the shrine was renamed Hachimangū. In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed back to Waguri Shrine and became a village shrine, but in {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806}} (Meiji 13) it was again renamed Waguri Shrine Hachimangū. In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658}} (Shōwa 10), it became a Prefectural Shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|lt=Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto}} *: A prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841}}. It is said that when Emperor Kōshō stayed in this area, he took as consort {{ill|lt=Yotsugi Tarashi-hime|Q10869205|WD=Q10869205}}, the daughter of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshin-O no Mikoto|Q97613650|WD=Q97613650|lt=Okitsu Yoae no Kimi}}, a local powerful figure. Their child was Ame-oshitara-hiko. * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997}} === Festivals === * Chigo Festival (May 5) * Annual Festival (October 20) === Auxiliary shrines === * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine – One of the candidate sites (ronsha) for '''Kawashima Shrine''' of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}. * Kotohira Shrine * Heiwa Shrine === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Ichinomiya City === * Colored portrait on paper of Kanematsu Masakichi * Three donated swords === Transportation === * Get off at the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397}} “Waguri Shrine-mae” bus stop; the shrine is just a short walk away. ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838}} or {{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|WD=Q11235139}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679}}, take a bus bound for “Kawashima” from platform 3 of the Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakurishinsha}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

'ja'} [[JA:若栗神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616546}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=October 26, 2014 (Sun) 01:50 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Waguri Shrine | Image = [[File:Waguri Shrine Hachimangu Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}} Shimamura Minami-Urayama 75 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|lt=Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} · Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | Established = Unknown | Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Waguri Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|thumbnail|250px|Torii gate and marker pillar]] [[File:Waguri Shrine Hachimangu Origin Record.jpg|thumb|250px|Origin record]] '''Waguri Shrine''' (わぐりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Shimamura (Ichinomiya City)|Q128848556|WD=Q128848556|lt=Shimamura|1=Shimamura}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was Prefectural Shrine. The official name is “Waguri Shrine Hachimangū.” This is because it once bore the name Hachimangū. However, it is more commonly called Waguri Shrine. It is the “Waguri Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. === Overview === The founding date is said to be during the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}}, when a local powerful figure of this region, Haguri no Omi Hitomaro, established the shrine to enshrine his ancestor (Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto). During the {{ill|lt=Tenbun era|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|1=Tenbun}}, a Buddhist statue ({{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}) was jointly enshrined, and in {{ill|lt=1605|Q6741|WD=Q6741|1=1605}} (Keichō 10), the domain lord Kanematsu Masakichi (a retainer of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186|WD=Q1136186|1=Tokugawa Yoshinao}} of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}) formally enshrined Hachimangū, and the shrine was renamed Hachimangū. In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed back to Waguri Shrine and became a village shrine, but in {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13) it was again renamed Waguri Shrine Hachimangū. In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} (Shōwa 10), it became a Prefectural Shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|lt=Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}} *: A prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}. It is said that when Emperor Kōshō stayed in this area, he took as consort {{ill|lt=Yotsugi Tarashi-hime|Q10869205|WD=Q10869205|1=Yosotarashi-hime}}, the daughter of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshin-O no Mikoto|Q97613650|WD=Q97613650|lt=Okitsu Yoae no Kimi|1=Amaoshio no mikoto}}, a local powerful figure. Their child was Ame-oshitara-hiko. * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === Festivals === * Chigo Festival (May 5) * Annual Festival (October 20) === Auxiliary shrines === * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine – One of the candidate sites (ronsha) for '''Kawashima Shrine''' of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. * Kotohira Shrine * Heiwa Shrine === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Ichinomiya City === * Colored portrait on paper of Kanematsu Masakichi * Three donated swords === Transportation === * Get off at the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}} “Waguri Shrine-mae” bus stop; the shrine is just a short walk away. ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} or {{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|WD=Q11235139|1=Tōkaidō Line (JR Central)}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}, take a bus bound for “Kawashima” from platform 3 of the Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Wakurishinsha}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

'ja'} [[JA:若栗神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616546}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=October 26, 2014 (Sun) 01:50 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Waguri Shrine | Image = [[File:Waguri Shrine Hachimangu Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}} Shimamura Minami-Urayama 75 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|lt=Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} · Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | Established = Unknown | Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Waguri Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|thumbnail|250px|Torii gate and marker pillar]] [[File:Waguri Shrine Hachimangu Origin Record.jpg|thumb|250px|Origin record]] '''Waguri Shrine''' (わぐりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Shimamura (Ichinomiya City)|Q128848556|WD=Q128848556|lt=Shimamura|1=Shimamura}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was Prefectural Shrine. The official name is “Waguri Shrine Hachimangū.” This is because it once bore the name Hachimangū. However, it is more commonly called Waguri Shrine. It is the “Waguri Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. === Overview === The founding date is said to be during the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}}, when a local powerful figure of this region, Haguri no Omi Hitomaro, established the shrine to enshrine his ancestor (Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto). During the {{ill|lt=Tenbun era|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|1=Tenbun}}, a Buddhist statue ({{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}) was jointly enshrined, and in {{ill|lt=1605|Q6741|WD=Q6741|1=1605}} (Keichō 10), the domain lord Kanematsu Masakichi (a retainer of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186|WD=Q1136186|1=Tokugawa Yoshinao}} of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}) formally enshrined Hachimangū, and the shrine was renamed Hachimangū. In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed back to Waguri Shrine and became a village shrine, but in {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13) it was again renamed Waguri Shrine Hachimangū. In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} (Shōwa 10), it became a Prefectural Shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|lt=Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}} *: A prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}. It is said that when Emperor Kōshō stayed in this area, he took as consort {{ill|lt=Yotsugi Tarashi-hime|Q10869205|WD=Q10869205|1=Yosotarashi-hime}}, the daughter of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshin-O no Mikoto|Q97613650|WD=Q97613650|lt=Okitsu Yoae no Kimi|1=Amaoshio no mikoto}}, a local powerful figure. Their child was Ame-oshitara-hiko. * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === Festivals === * Chigo Festival (May 5) * Annual Festival (October 20) === Auxiliary shrines === * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine – One of the candidate sites (ronsha) for '''Kawashima Shrine''' of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. * Kotohira Shrine * Heiwa Shrine === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Ichinomiya City === * Colored portrait on paper of Kanematsu Masakichi * Three donated swords === Transportation === * Get off at the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}} “Waguri Shrine-mae” bus stop; the shrine is just a short walk away. ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} or {{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|WD=Q11235139|1=Tōkaidō Line (JR Central)}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}, take a bus bound for “Kawashima” from platform 3 of the Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:八幡宮]]

'ja'} [[JA:若栗神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616546}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=October 26, 2014 (Sun) 01:50 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Waguri Shrine | Image = [[File:Waguri Shrine Hachimangu Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}} Shimamura Minami-Urayama 75 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|lt=Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} · Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | Established = Unknown | Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Waguri Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|thumbnail|250px|Torii gate and marker pillar]] [[File:Waguri Shrine Hachimangu Origin Record.jpg|thumb|250px|Origin record]] '''Waguri Shrine''' (わぐりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Shimamura (Ichinomiya City)|Q128848556|WD=Q128848556|lt=Shimamura|1=Shimamura}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was Prefectural Shrine. The official name is “Waguri Shrine Hachimangū.” This is because it once bore the name Hachimangū. However, it is more commonly called Waguri Shrine. It is the “Waguri Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. === Overview === The founding date is said to be during the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}}, when a local powerful figure of this region, Haguri no Omi Hitomaro, established the shrine to enshrine his ancestor (Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto). During the {{ill|lt=Tenbun era|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|1=Tenbun}}, a Buddhist statue ({{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}) was jointly enshrined, and in {{ill|lt=1605|Q6741|WD=Q6741|1=1605}} (Keichō 10), the domain lord Kanematsu Masakichi (a retainer of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186|WD=Q1136186|1=Tokugawa Yoshinao}} of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}) formally enshrined Hachimangū, and the shrine was renamed Hachimangū. In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed back to Waguri Shrine and became a village shrine, but in {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13) it was again renamed Waguri Shrine Hachimangū. In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} (Shōwa 10), it became a Prefectural Shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|lt=Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}} *: A prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}. It is said that when Emperor Kōshō stayed in this area, he took as consort {{ill|lt=Yotsugi Tarashi-hime|Q10869205|WD=Q10869205|1=Yosotarashi-hime}}, the daughter of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshin-O no Mikoto|Q97613650|WD=Q97613650|lt=Okitsu Yoae no Kimi|1=Amaoshio no mikoto}}, a local powerful figure. Their child was Ame-oshitara-hiko. * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === Festivals === * Chigo Festival (May 5) * Annual Festival (October 20) === Auxiliary shrines === * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine – One of the candidate sites (ronsha) for '''Kawashima Shrine''' of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. * Kotohira Shrine * Heiwa Shrine === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Ichinomiya City === * Colored portrait on paper of Kanematsu Masakichi * Three donated swords === Transportation === * Get off at the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}} “Waguri Shrine-mae” bus stop; the shrine is just a short walk away. ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} or {{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|WD=Q11235139|1=Tōkaidō Line (JR Central)}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}, take a bus bound for “Kawashima” from platform 3 of the Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:愛知県の旧県社]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]]

Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|1=Ken-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|WD=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|WD=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * わぐりじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 1180005010190 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県一宮市島村南裏山75', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:若栗神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11616546}} == Japanese content == {{Citation needed|date=October 26, 2014 (Sun) 01:50 (UTC)}} {{Shinto shrine | Name = Waguri Shrine | Image = [[File:Waguri Shrine Hachimangu Haiden.jpg|250px]]
Haiden (worship hall) | Location = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}} Shimamura Minami-Urayama 75 | Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} | Enshrined deities = {{ill|lt=Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|lt=Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} · Former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Prefectural Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} | Established = Unknown | Map = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Waguri Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Waguri-jinja, Ichinomiya.jpg|thumbnail|250px|Torii gate and marker pillar]] [[File:Waguri Shrine Hachimangu Origin Record.jpg|thumb|250px|Origin record]] '''Waguri Shrine''' (わぐりじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Shimamura (Ichinomiya City)|Q128848556|WD=Q128848556|lt=Shimamura|1=Shimamura}}, {{ill|lt=Ichinomiya City|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. Its former {{ill|lt=shrine rank|Q10444029|WD=Q10444029|1=Shakaku}} was Prefectural Shrine. The official name is “Waguri Shrine Hachimangū.” This is because it once bore the name Hachimangū. However, it is more commonly called Waguri Shrine. It is the “Waguri Shrine” of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. === Overview === The founding date is said to be during the {{ill|lt=Hakuhō period|Q11580612|WD=Q11580612|1=Hakuhō}}, when a local powerful figure of this region, Haguri no Omi Hitomaro, established the shrine to enshrine his ancestor (Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto). During the {{ill|lt=Tenbun era|Q1192853|WD=Q1192853|1=Tenbun}}, a Buddhist statue ({{ill|lt=Hachiman deity|Q261637|WD=Q261637|1=Hachiman}}) was jointly enshrined, and in {{ill|lt=1605|Q6741|WD=Q6741|1=1605}} (Keichō 10), the domain lord Kanematsu Masakichi (a retainer of {{ill|lt=Tokugawa Yoshinao|Q1136186|WD=Q1136186|1=Tokugawa Yoshinao}} of the {{ill|lt=Owari Domain|Q1062072|WD=Q1062072|1=Owari Domain}}) formally enshrined Hachimangū, and the shrine was renamed Hachimangū. In {{ill|lt=1869|Q7720|WD=Q7720|1=1869}} (Meiji 2), it was renamed back to Waguri Shrine and became a village shrine, but in {{ill|lt=1880|Q7806|WD=Q7806|1=1880}} (Meiji 13) it was again renamed Waguri Shrine Hachimangū. In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} (Shōwa 10), it became a Prefectural Shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|lt=Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}} *: A prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}. It is said that when Emperor Kōshō stayed in this area, he took as consort {{ill|lt=Yotsugi Tarashi-hime|Q10869205|WD=Q10869205|1=Yosotarashi-hime}}, the daughter of {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Oshin-O no Mikoto|Q97613650|WD=Q97613650|lt=Okitsu Yoae no Kimi|1=Amaoshio no mikoto}}, a local powerful figure. Their child was Ame-oshitara-hiko. * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === Festivals === * Chigo Festival (May 5) * Annual Festival (October 20) === Auxiliary shrines === * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine – One of the candidate sites (ronsha) for '''Kawashima Shrine''' of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. * Kotohira Shrine * Heiwa Shrine === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Ichinomiya City === * Colored portrait on paper of Kanematsu Masakichi * Three donated swords === Transportation === * Get off at the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}} “Waguri Shrine-mae” bus stop; the shrine is just a short walk away. ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} or {{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|WD=Q11235139|1=Tōkaidō Line (JR Central)}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}, take a bus bound for “Kawashima” from platform 3 of the Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Hachiman shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]]

In {{ill|lt=1935|Q18658|WD=Q18658|1=1935}} (Shōwa 10), it became a Prefectural Shrine. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ame-tarashihiko-kunioshi-hito no Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|lt=Ame-oshitara-hiko no Mikoto|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}} *: A prince of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōshō|Q312841|WD=Q312841|1=Emperor Kōshō}}. Their child was Ame-oshitara-hiko. * {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|WD=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}} === Festivals === * Chigo Festival (May 5) * Annual Festival (October 20) === Auxiliary shrines === * Kawashima Tenjin Shrine – One of the candidate sites (ronsha) for '''Kawashima Shrine''' of {{ill|lt=Haguri District|Q1032507|WD=Q1032507|1=Haguri District}}, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. * Kotohira Shrine * Heiwa Shrine === Cultural properties === === Designated cultural properties of Ichinomiya City === * Colored portrait on paper of Kanematsu Masakichi * Three donated swords === Transportation === * Get off at the {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Bus|Q6810397|WD=Q6810397|1=Meitetsu Bus}} “Waguri Shrine-mae” bus stop; the shrine is just a short walk away. ** From {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Main Line|Q1137838|WD=Q1137838|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}} or {{ill|lt=Bisai Line|Q901005|WD=Q901005|1=Meitetsu Bisai Line}}, or from {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tōkaidō Main Line (Nagoya area)|Q11235139|WD=Q11235139|1=Tōkaidō Line (JR Central)}} of {{ill|lt=Central Japan Railway Company|Q513679|WD=Q513679|1=Central Japan Railway Company}}, take a bus bound for “Kawashima” from platform 3 of the Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station Bus Terminal. === Notes === {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Waguri Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ichinomiya, Aichi]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]]
